summary: The initiative delivers an offline, single‑HTML‑file Sudoku web game for casual puzzle players. It supports three difficulty levels, a responsive UI, timer, mistake counter, pause/resume, and undo/redo. The code is structured to allow future addition of a puzzle generator while remaining fully client‑side.

assumptions.text: We assume the curated puzzle list will be embedded directly in the HTML file rather than fetched from an external source.
We assume the UI will be built with vanilla JavaScript/HTML/CSS without any third‑party UI frameworks.

requirementsInterpreted.text: Users can select a difficulty (easy, medium, hard) and load a new valid Sudoku puzzle.
The 9x9 grid displays fixed starting numbers that cannot be edited and editable cells for user input.
Users can select a cell and enter numbers 1‑9 via keyboard or on‑screen controls, with a mistake counter incrementing on rule violations.
The game highlights the selected cell’s row, column, and 3x3 box, and optionally flags duplicate numbers.
A timer runs during play, can be paused/resumed, and the pause screen blanks the game view.
Undo and redo actions revert and reapply user moves without affecting fixed cells or timer state.

outOfScope.text: Persistent user accounts, cloud save, or any server‑side storage.
Integration with external APIs or services for puzzle generation.
Advanced hint or solving assistance beyond basic validation.

openQuestions.text: Is there a limit on the number of curated puzzles that can be embedded, affecting the maximum size of the single HTML file?
Should the undo/redo stack be unlimited or capped at a specific number of moves?

riskFlags.text: The single‑file offline constraint prevents fetching additional puzzles at runtime.
Prohibiting external libraries may increase development effort to achieve a polished UI.
Implementing undo/redo adds significant complexity to the game engine.
Lack of automated test coverage for edge cases could lead to undetected bugs.
